name: Parque Natural de las Salinas de Santa Pola – Circular Walk
name_original: Parque Natural de las Salinas de Santa Pola – Paseo circular
region_intro: Touring cycling around Santa Pola offers diverse landscapes along Spain's Costa Blanca. The region features over 11 kilometers of coastline with generally smooth gradients, making it accessible for various fitness levels. Cyclists can explore the unique salt flats of the Parque Natural de las Salinas, pine forests within the Sierra de Santa Pola, and coastal roads providing views of the Mediterranean Sea. The terrain includes dedicated bike paths, paved circuits, and some unpaved segments. Best touring cycling routes around Santa Pola The most popular touring cycling route is Parque Natural de las Salinas de Santa Pola – Circular Walk, a 17.1 miles (27.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 49 minutes to complete. This easy route explores the unique salt flats, offering opportunities for wildlife observation. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Old Road to Santa Pola – Little Coves of the Cape loop from Urbanización Don Pueblo Fase II, a moderate 20.3 miles (32.7 km) path. This route features coastal views and passes by small coves along the old road. Local touring cyclists also love the Rambla de Santa Pola – Santa Pola Fishing Harbour loop from Urbanización Don Pueblo Fase II, a 33.4 miles (53.8 km) trail leading through coastal areas and past the vibrant fishing harbor, often completed in about 3 hours 23 minutes. Touring cycling around Santa Pola is defined by coastal paths, salt flats, and pine forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy, flat routes to more challenging ascents in the Sierra de Santa Pola. The routes in Santa Pola are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 300 reviews. More than 1,900 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Santa Pola's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Santa Pola?
A: Santa Pola offers a wide variety of touring cycling routes, with over 70 options available. These routes cater to different skill levels, from easy coastal paths to more challenging ascents in the Sierra de Santa Pola.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Santa Pola?
A: You can expect diverse terrain, including over 11 kilometers of coastline with generally smooth gradients, dedicated bike paths, and paved circuits. The region also features unique salt flats in the Parque Natural de las Salinas, pine forests within the Sierra de Santa Pola, and some unpaved segments for varied experiences.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Santa Pola?
A: The touring cycling routes in Santa Pola are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 300 reviews. Cyclists frequently praise the varied terrain, the stunning coastal views, and the opportunities for wildlife observation, especially in the natural parks.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Santa Pola?
A: Yes, Santa Pola is very accessible for beginners. There are over 30 easy routes, many of which feature smooth slopes and dedicated bike paths. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e952438365/parque-natural-de-las-salinas-de-santa-pola-circular-walk" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural de las Salinas de Santa Pola – Circular Walk&lt;/a&gt;, which is 17.1 miles (27.5 km) and explores the unique salt flats.

Q: Where can I find more challenging touring cycling routes in Santa Pola?
A: For those seeking more challenging rides, the Sierra de Santa Pola offers routes with ascents through pine forests, providing impressive panoramic views. While most routes are easy to moderate, there are a few more demanding options that explore the varied topography of the region.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Santa Pola?
A: Yes, many routes in Santa Pola are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e952438365/parque-natural-de-las-salinas-de-santa-pola-circular-walk" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural de las Salinas de Santa Pola – Circular Walk&lt;/a&gt;. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40385557" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rambla de Santa Pola – Old Road to Santa Pola loop from Santa Pola&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 18.2 miles (29.3 km) ride.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling routes in Santa Pola?
A: Santa Pola is an excellent destination for family cycling. Many routes feature smooth, gentle gradients and dedicated bike paths, making them suitable for all ages. The coastal roads and paths through the natural park are particularly popular for family outings.

Q: What natural attractions can I see while cycling in Santa Pola?
A: While cycling, you can explore the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7113457" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Pola Salt Flats Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, home to diverse flora and fauna, including migratory birds like flamingos. You might also pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4092860" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Anguilla Observatory&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4364640" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Entrance to the Parc Natural de les Salines de Santa Pola&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural sights to visit along the touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, you can incorporate cultural stops into your rides. The 16th-century Castle-Fortress of Santa Pola offers historical insights. You might also cycle past the vibrant Fishing Port or explore nearby attractions like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2531293" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Elche&lt;/a&gt;, which features the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4391974" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Altamira Palace&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4392051" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Elche Municipal Park&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Where can I find scenic viewpoints on the touring cycling routes?
A: For breathtaking views, head towards the Sierra de Santa Pola. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/354465" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Pola Lighthouse and Viewing Walkway&lt;/a&gt; offers spectacular panoramic views of Tabarca Island and stunning sunsets. Coastal roads, such as those on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33743705" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Road to Santa Pola – Little Coves of the Cape loop&lt;/a&gt;, also provide beautiful sea vistas.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Santa Pola?
A: Santa Pola's mild Mediterranean climate makes it suitable for touring cycling almost year-round. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures, avoiding the peak heat of summer while still providing plenty of sunshine. Winter can also be a great time for cycling, with cooler but often sunny days.

Q: Are there opportunities for wildlife spotting on the touring cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7113457" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Pola Salt Flats Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; is a prime location for birdwatching, especially for migratory birds like flamingos, particularly around the Tamarit Tower. You can also visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4036358" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torre El Rincón Bird Observatory&lt;/a&gt; for dedicated viewing.

Q: Are there places to stop for refreshments along the routes?
A: Many routes in Santa Pola pass through or near urban areas and coastal towns, offering numerous opportunities to stop for refreshments. You'll find cafes, restaurants, and bars, especially around the fishing harbor and along the beaches, perfect for a break and to sample local cuisine.
tips: The Palm Tree Trail runs through part of the Santa Pola Salt Flats Natural Park, between dunes and ancient salt flats. It's an easy path that combines walkways and viewpoints, perfect for leisurely strolls. Along the way, you can observe flamingos and other birds that inhabit this unique ecosystem.
Top flair! Very beautiful coastline surrounded by dunes. The houses are directly on the sea, but are accessible.
